w:r.w:t: Identify the top 10 websites, blogs, or directories that link to or mention '
' specifically in the context of Connecticut real estate. For each site found, tell me:
The domain name and its relevance to LGBTQ+ housing.
Whether it's a directory, a news site, or a blog.
Search for a 'Contact Us' or 'Submit a Link' page for each one
specifically in the Connecticut LGBTQ+ real estate context, along with contact/submission details for each.
The primary national LGBTQ+ real estate agent directory with a dedicated
— includes a contact form and toll-free number 1-888-420-MOVE (6683)
The National Association of Gay & Lesbian Real Estate Professionals; has a dedicated
listing gay realtors in cities like New Canaan, Milford, and Stamford
lists partner links — contact via the site's member/join page
, listing Connecticut gay real estate agents such as Luke Broughton (Greenwich) with full profile data
No dedicated "Submit a Link" page found; contact likely via the agent submission form at
Another dedicated LGBTQ+ realtor directory with a
listing agents in Fairfield County and surrounding areas
Contact and agent listing submission available through the site's main menu at
Connecticut-specific LGBTQ+ real estate network connecting buyers with vetted agents, lenders, and attorneys in Hartford, New Haven, and Litchfield
Directory / Blog (includes blog posts on LGBTQ+-inclusive school districts)
A Connecticut-focused LGBTQ+ realtor site with local LGBTQ+ CT resources; linked from Yale's LGBTQ Strong directory for realtor Brennan Mohring
Contact page likely accessible via the site's navigation at
An LGBTQ+ lifestyle and news magazine that directly mentions and links to
, covering real estate and community news in Connecticut and the Northeast
Reachable through the magazine's editorial/contact page at
Official CT LGBTQ+ business chamber with a real estate professional directory listing agents like Mike Daly (Hartford area) — a natural referral source for both
Yale University's LGBTQ resource directory that directly links to
and lists Connecticut LGBTQ+ real estate agents like Brennan Mohring
Contact Yale's LGBTQ Strong program through
Connecticut-based LGBTQ+ resource hub with a dedicated LGBTQIA+ resources section that covers housing equity, homelessness prevention, and links to LGBTQ+ housing support services
Resource submission or link addition likely via the contact page at
For your SEO work on the LGBTQ+ real estate site,
are the highest-authority backlink opportunities, as institutional and chamber links carry strong domain trust.
is the best editorial/press outreach target since it has already published content directly linking to

Find the most active and reputable LGBTQ+ community organizations, pride event organizers, and local news outlets (like CT Insider or Hartford Courant) that have sections or articles dedicated to 'LGBTQ+ living in Connecticut'. List the specific URLs for their 'Resource' pages or 'Community Partners' sections where a professional real estate network might be listed

Here is a comprehensive breakdown of the most active and reputable LGBTQ+ organizations, pride event organizers, and local news outlets in Connecticut — along with the specific URLs for their resource, community partners, or submission pages.
Connecticut's leading queer-led grassroots advocacy organization, focused on advancing LGBTQIA+ rights, housing equity, culture, and legislation statewide. Their
explicitly collects links for community leaders, educators, and LGBTQIA+ individuals — a prime place for a real estate network listing.
Fairfield County's largest LGBTQ+ nonprofit (est. 1990), offering 30+ programs and services including housing navigation support. Their homepage explicitly has a
section, and they actively list partner organizations.
Greater New Haven's LGBTQ+ hub providing advocacy, support services, and community programs. Their site explicitly invites organizations to
— a direct opportunity to list an LGBTQ+ real estate network.
A statewide LGBTQIA+ resource hub covering mental health, housing, and community referrals. Their
links to vetted organizations and housing support services.
5. Connecticut Gay & Lesbian Chamber of Commerce —
The premier LGBTQ+ business networking organization in CT with 165+ members across all industries including real estate. Their
is exactly where a real estate professional network would be listed.

The official state government LGBTQ+ services directory maintained by CT's Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services. It links to partner organizations across housing, health, and legal resources.
public submission or relevant state agency

An active pride organization providing resources, grants, education, and free events for Connecticut's LGBTQIA+ community. They actively collaborate with community partners and list them on their site.
A volunteer-driven pride group serving Greater Danbury and Western CT, fiscally sponsored by Triangle Community Center. They partner with businesses and community groups and list community partners.

, published quarterly and distributed free to 7,500+ households and at LGBTQ+ events statewide. They cover real estate, business, and community features and offer advertising/partnership opportunities.

Connecticut's public media outlet that runs LGBTQ+ community guides (including an annual CT Pride event guide) and covers housing equity and LGBTQ+ living topics. Getting mentioned in a CT Public feature or resource article provides strong editorial credibility.
Via CT Public's editorial/tip submission at
(they explicitly invite partners via email), and
(media exposure to 12,500+ LGBTQ+ readers). These three alone can generate quality backlinks and referral traffic for your client's site.

Act as a researcher for a gay couple moving from NYC to West Hartford, CT. Search the web and provide a list of the 5 most reliable sources for finding LGBTQ+ friendly real estate agents and attorneys in CT. Specifically check if '
' appears in any recent search results or AI summaries. If it does not appear, explain what sources the current results are prioritizing.
in current search results. Here is the full researcher's briefing for a gay couple relocating from NYC to West Hartford, CT.
Yes — it appears directly in search results
for queries like "LGBTQ+ friendly real estate agents Connecticut." The site appears twice: once on its homepage and once on its
"Connecting the LGBTQ+ community with trusted, inclusive real estate professionals in Connecticut. Agents, lenders, and attorneys — all in one place."
It lists services specifically for Hartford, New Haven, and Litchfield — and even highlights
relocation services from NYC to CT
, which is exactly the use case for this couple.
The 5 Most Reliable Sources for LGBTQ+ Realtors & Attorneys in CT

Connecticut's only dedicated, locally-focused LGBTQ+ real estate network, listing vetted agents, Top 1% mortgage lenders, and real estate attorneys in Hartford County — directly relevant for West Hartford buyers. It also explicitly offers NYC-to-CT relocation services.
The national LGBTQ+ real estate directory has a
listing agents like Gerard (central CT specialist) and Arek, with verified credentials and reviews. It's the highest-traffic national directory and its CT listings are actively maintained.
Vetted individual agent profiles with reviews

The National Association of Gay & Lesbian Real Estate Professionals lists verified member agents across CT cities, with one agent citing being the #8 RE/MAX agent in CT in 2024 with 75–100 annual closings. This is the most professionally credentialed network for serious buyers.
4. Super Lawyers — LGBTQ+ Attorneys in Hartford/West Hartford
For real estate and family law attorneys, Super Lawyers'

lists rated attorneys like Greg C. Mogel and Sandi B. Girolamo — both based in West Hartford — along with Kathleen Nevins and Joshua Feldman in Hartford proper. These are peer-reviewed and state bar verified.
Finding a vetted LGBTQ+-experienced real estate or family law attorney

A curated LGBTQ+ real estate directory with detailed Connecticut agent profiles, including buyer relocation specialists and agents serving Fairfield County and Hartford regions. Profiles include the types of LGBTQ+ clientele each agent welcomes (gay men, lesbian, non-binary, transgender).
Filtering agents by specific identity and region
What Sources Are Currently Being Prioritized?
Bottom line for your client's site:
is indexed and showing in results — which is a solid foundation. The gap vs.
, not content quality. Targeting backlinks from the organizations listed in the previous research (
